prompt: The lexer fails to handle multi-character operators like >= and != - test_comparison_ops panics

turns.content:
<task>
The lexer fails to handle multi-character operators like >= and != - test_comparison_ops panics
</task>

test tests::test_comparison_ops ... FAILED

---- tests::test_comparison_ops stdout ----
thread 'tests::test_comparison_ops' panicked at 'assertion failed: `(left == right)`
  left: `[Gt, Eq, Bang, Eq]`,
 right: `[Gte, Neq]`', src/lexer.rs:72:9

arguments.reasoning: The lexer handles >, <, !, = as single characters but doesn't check for the following = to form >=, <=, !=. I need to add lookahead for these multi-character operators. When seeing >, check if next char is =, if so emit Gte and advance 2. Same for < (Lte) and ! (Neq).

arguments.summary: Bug: lexer handles >, <, ! as single tokens without lookahead for = to form >=, <=, !=. Test expects [Gte, Neq] but gets [Gt, Eq, Bang, Eq].

next_steps.description: Add lookahead for '>' checking next char is '=' to emit Gte (advance 2), otherwise Gt. Same for '<' -> Lte and '!' -> Neq
Run cargo test to verify comparison operators are correctly tokenized

turns.content:
running 3 tests
test tests::test_basic_tokens ... ok
test tests::test_comparison_ops ... ok
test tests::test_numbers ... ok

test result: ok. 3 passed; 0 failed

arguments.summary: Added lookahead for multi-character operators: >=, <=, != now correctly tokenized as Gte, Lte, Neq. Single >, <, ! still work when not followed by =. All 3 tests pass.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed lexer in src/lexer.rs: added lookahead for multi-character operators. When seeing > / < / !, checks if next char is = to emit Gte/Lte/Neq (advancing 2), otherwise emits single-char token. All 3 tests pass.
